steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence claims the pseudonym "accomplished far more than simply concealing his authorship." The second sentence reinforces that point: it was a complex rhetorical strategy, not an arbitrary pen name. The transition must signal confirmation and intensification of the same idea.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Indeed': signals confirmation and intensification — exactly the relationship between the two sentences. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Conversely': signals a contrast or reversal. The second sentence does not contradict the first. ✗
  • Choice C — 'However': also signals contrast. Same problem as "Conversely." ✗
  • Choice D — 'In addition': introduces a new, separate point. The second sentence restates and deepens the first rather than adding a distinct idea. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
